meta.name: Basmati Rice (Fluffy Stovetop Method)
meta.subtitle: Rinse + soak, then gentle simmer and steam for separate grains

source.name: SSA canonical cuisines/indian/recipes/basmati_rice.json
notes: Deterministic baseline basmati rice suitable for Indian meals (dal, curries, kebabs).
Includes soak option, absorption method, and common variations (jeera rice, ghee rice).
Written to be friendly to SSA planners: weeknight, batch, leftovers, and reheating guidance.

summary: A reliable stovetop basmati rice method for fluffy, separate grains. Rinse well, optionally soak, then simmer briefly and steam covered off heat. Perfect with dal and curries.

optional.label: Rice cooker
optional.notes: Variation included; use cooker instructions with adjusted water if needed.

signals.freezerFriendly: yes (portion and freeze; reheat with steam)

notes: Rice is naturally gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Basmati rice (white), rinsed
notes: Rinse until water runs mostly clear to reduce surface starch and improve fluffy grains.
For extra long, separate grains, soak 15–20 minutes after rinsing (optional).

ingredients.label: Water
notes: Baseline absorption ratio for rinsed basmati: 1 cup rice : 1.5 cups water.
If you skip rinsing (not recommended), you may need slightly less water to avoid gumminess.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Optional, but recommended for rice served with dal/curry.

ingredients.label: Ghee or butter (optional)
notes: Optional for richer rice (ghee rice).
For vegan, omit or use neutral oil.

steps.title: Rinse the rice
instructions: Place basmati rice in a bowl and cover with cool water. Swish with your hand.
Pour off cloudy water and repeat 3–5 times, or until the water is mostly clear.
Drain well in a fine mesh strainer.
checks: Clearer rinse water = less surface starch = fluffier rice.
Drain thoroughly so your water ratio stays accurate.

steps.title: Optional soak (for extra separate grains)
instructions: Optional: return drained rice to the bowl, cover with fresh water, and soak 15–20 minutes.
checks: Soaking improves elongation and even cooking.
Always drain before cooking to keep your ratio consistent.

steps.title: Bring to a boil
instructions: Add rinsed (and drained) rice to a pot with measured water and salt.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat.
checks: Use a pot with a tight-fitting lid for best steaming.

steps.title: Simmer covered until water is absorbed
instructions: Once boiling, stir once, then reduce heat to low.
Cover with a tight lid and simmer 12 minutes (do not lift the lid).
checks: Keeping the lid closed traps steam and ensures even cooking.
Heat should be low enough to prevent scorching.

steps.title: Steam off heat, then fluff
instructions: Turn off heat. Keep lid on and let rice steam 10 minutes.
Fluff gently with a fork to separate grains.
checks: The rest is what finishes the grains without breaking them.
Fluff with a fork, not a spoon, to avoid mashing.

notes: Also excellent with grilled meats and roasted vegetables.
Cool quickly and store airtight.
Reheat with steam: sprinkle 1–2 tbsp water per cup rice, cover, and warm gently.
Freeze in flat portions for quick reheating.
Microwave from frozen with a splash of water, covered, until hot.
Cooked rice can harbor Bacillus cereus if left warm too long—cool promptly and refrigerate within 2 hours.
Reheat until steaming hot.
Before adding water, warm 1 tbsp ghee/oil and bloom 1 tsp cumin seeds for 20–30 seconds.
Add rice and stir 30 seconds, then add water + salt and proceed as written.
Use 1–2 tbsp ghee; optionally add 2 green cardamom pods and a small cinnamon stick while simmering.
Remove whole spices before serving.
Rinse rice well. Use ~1.35–1.5 cups water per 1 cup rice depending on cooker and desired firmness.
Cook on white rice setting; rest 10 minutes on warm, then fluff.
Use 1 cup brown basmati to 2 cups water; simmer 35–45 minutes then rest 10 minutes.
Brown rice is chewier; plan more total time.
Omit salt; rely on dal/curry sauces for seasoning.
